mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects consumer spending momentum that directly impacts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 5-day forward returns is negative or below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window, labor market deterioration triggers earnings recession fears and risk-off flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of cases over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of accelerating consumer prices and restrictive Fed policy creates a stagflationary discount where rising CPIAUCSL combined with elevated FEDFUNDS compresses real earnings growth expectations more than either factor alone, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS levels shows no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) for 60-day returns in a walk-forward regression, or the combined signal underperforms the univariate FEDFUNDS signal.

mechanism_narrative: During housing-led expansions, month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal construction multiplier effects and durable goods demand, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth is above 2% annualized and HOUST is rising, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) across a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth (INDPRO) captures capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum that translates into near-term earnings surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO changes exhibit negative rank correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur less than 52% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect expanding employment that supports consumption forecasts and reduces recession probability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the labor market anchor validates equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive financial conditions dominate earnings optimism, and further rate increases or sustained plateau levels produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over a full walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of weakening retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) creates a double-drag on consumption and corporate pricing power that amplifies downside equity risk beyond either single indicator, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal of falling RSAFS and rising UNRATE does not produce negative 5-day returns in > 55% of joint-triggered instances, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression over a 3-year window.
